feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Extended Warehouse Management
feed.subtitle: SAP Extended Warehouse Management Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: WMS API Equivalents in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition vs. SAP Business ByDesign
entry.content:
We are currently working with a customer who uses a .NET-based solution integrated with SAP Business ByDesign.

In the current ByD setup, warehouse operators work based on logistics tasks generated by the system (e.g., Goods Receipt, Stock Transfer, Picking). Our WMS consumes these tasks and allows operators to execute and confirm them. This process is supported through APIs such as SiteLogisticsTask and WarehouseConfirmation.

The customer is now evaluating a transition to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and would like to maintain the same operational model.

 

While we understand that S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition provides Warehouse Management capabilities and supports API-based integration with external systems, we are struggling to identify the equivalent of the ByD logistics task concept.

 

Our questions are:

  • Is there an equivalent to ByD Logistics Tasks / Warehouse Tasks in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition?
  • Can these tasks be created, retrieved, processed, and confirmed through standard APIs by an external WMS?
  • Which business objects and APIs should be considered as the equivalent of SiteLogisticsTask and WarehouseConfirmation in ByD?
  • Are there any limitations or recommended approaches when implementing a task-driven warehouse execution process with an external WMS in Public Cloud?

Our objective is to offer the customer the same warehouse execution experience they currently have in ByD while following SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition best practices and Clean Core principles.

Any guidance, architecture recommendations, or references to relevant APIs and business objects would be greatly appreciated.

entry.title: Packaging Material check for Plant and status
entry.content:
Hello, 

Pack. Mat. N029 is not created in plant xxxx (MMSC, MM60, MM03 with all views, no stock levels and no warehouse tasks ever exist), I can change and save an existing pack specification in plant xxxx with pack material N029! 

Is there any possiblitiy that in T-code: /scwm/packspec the Pachaging Material is been crosschecked and thrown an error.

  1. if its been in the plant existance?
  2. if the packaging material has an active status?

 

BR

Ibo

entry.title: SERIAL NUMBER MANAGEMENT ERROR
entry.content:
FACING THIS ERROR WHILE DOING PUTAWAY OF SERIALIZED MATERIALS ,
SERIAL STATUS B AT ITEM LEVEL POPPED
MAINTAINED NO OBLOGATORY OPTION 3 FOR ANY FIELDS IN SERIAL PROFILE 
NEW MATERIAL NEW PO AFTER CHANGES IN SERIAL PROFILE 

STILL THE SAME

entry.title: Split WT for HU does not work in bulk storage type
entry.content:
Dear SAP.

    Currenly we found an issue for WT creation and confirm WT for storage type "Bulk" which is copy from SAP Standard (S. type 004) during create WT refer to ODO which is not split WT by HU stock in Bulk stock bin and system required to confirm WT with whole Qty of WT which is not split by item of each HU.

In EWM WH 3011 --> Storage Type 3010 

Picking in Bulk Storage setting

The HU Picking Control =1 (Propose Source HU as Destination HU) and Round Whole Units = 2 (Round Down WT Quantity to Single Unit)

And  the Round Whole Units =1 (Storage Type Decides) in WPT settings 

Issue in client 240, ODO = DO30000013

Storage Type: 3010

Storage Bin: 3010-C03

And I have added document for your referrence as the attached.

Best regards,

Chalermpol.

entry.title: Picking confirmation error
entry.content:
Dear Support,

I have a problem, I attached for you the step by step of the three  mistake.

Reagard.

entry.title: In Which Scenarios Is POSC Truly Necessary?
entry.content:
If RF is already recording every movement, users are already making decisions, and Internal WPTs are controlling warehouse movements, then in what situations does POSC provide additional value?

I am trying to understand where experienced consultants see the biggest benefit of POSC beyond visibility and movement control.

entry.title: n Which Scenarios Is POSC Truly Necessary?
entry.content:
If RF is already recording every movement, users are already making decisions, and Internal WPTs are controlling warehouse movements, then in what situations does POSC provide additional value?

I am trying to understand where experienced consultants see the biggest benefit of POSC beyond visibility and movement control

entry.title: SAP EWM Automatic Warehouse Task creation Fails for Same line item contains HU and Loose material.
entry.content:
Title: Automatic WT Creation Issue for Mixed HU and Loose Stock from Same Inbound Delivery Item in SAP EWM

Hello Experts,

I am facing an issue with automatic Warehouse Task (WT) creation during inbound putaway in SAP EWM.

Scenario

  • Automatic WT creation is configured and working successfully.

  • A single Warehouse Process Type (WPT) is used for both HU-managed and loose stock.

  • Storage Type Search determines different destination storage types:

    • HU stock → HD Rack Storage Type

    • Loose stock → HL Rack Storage Type

  • Quantity Offsetting is configured to differentiate packed and loose quantities.

Expected Behavior

For an inbound delivery item containing both:

  • Packed quantity in an HU

  • Loose quantity outside the HU

the system should create:

> One WT for the HU stock to the HD Rack.

> One WT for the loose stock to the HL Rack.

Actual Behavior

When the inbound delivery item contains both HU and loose stock:

  • Automatic WT is created only for the HU Warehouse Request item.

  • No automatic WT is created for the loose Warehouse Request item.

  • The loose stock remains in the GR area and requires manual WT creation.

Additional Findings

  • If only loose stock is received, automatic WT creation works correctly.

  • If only HU stock is received, automatic WT creation works correctly.

  • The issue occurs only when HU and loose stock are received for the same inbound delivery item.

  • Manual WT creation for the loose stock works successfully and determines the correct destination storage type and bin.

  • SLG1 logs show PPF action evaluation messages, but no clear error indicating why WT creation is skipped for the loose Warehouse Request.

Question

Has anyone experienced a similar issue where automatic WT creation processes only the HU Warehouse Request item and skips the loose Warehouse Request item created from the same inbound delivery item?

Are there any known settings, PPF conditions, BAdIs, quantity offsetting limitations, or SAP Notes that should be checked in this scenario?

Any guidance would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you.

entry.title: Phys­i­cal Inven­tory Doc­u­ment – Unable to Change Party Enti­tled to Dis­pose Dur­ing Count­ing
entry.content:
Dear SAP Support,

We are facing an issue in SAP EWM Physical Inventory process.

  • We created a Physical Inventory Document successfully.
  • During document creation, the field “Party Entitled to Dispose (PED)” is populated (e.g., showing 04 parties).
  • However, during the counting process, the system does not allow us to change/update the Party Entitled to Dispose.

--- Steps to Reproduce ---

Create Physical Inventory Document

Observe multiple Party Entitled to Dispose assignedStart counting processTry to modify Party Entitled to DisposeSystem does not allow the change

entry.title: RF Confirmation for Picking from Mixed HU by Scanning Only the HU
entry.content:
I have a scenario where warehouse tasks are created for picking from a mixed handling unit (mixed HU).

The outbound delivery contains multiple items, and I need to pick these items from the same mixed HU. During warehouse task confirmation in RF, I would like the operator to confirm only the handling unit by scanning the HU barcode, without having to scan or confirm each individual product and quantity.

Is this scenario supported in standard SAP EWM? If so, which configuration or RF settings should be used to enable HU-only confirmation for picking from a mixed HU?

entry.title: Kit to Order without VAS ASR transport driven outbound process in EWM
entry.content:
Hello EWM & TM experts, 

I am setting up Kit to Order without VAS outbound process in EWM. This process need to be configured with ASR(Advanced shipping & receiving )  transport driven TM integration. ( Product version: S/4HANA  2025 EWM-TM embeded)

SAP has given standard OFO EWM document type  and ODFO item type which support ASR transport driven scenario.

However I dont find EWM item type similiar to OKC and OKM which support ASR process for kit to order scenario.

I tried to modify the status profile /SCDL/OUT_PRD_DLV_KTO of the OKM item type to make it ASR relevant however the status field are different in status profile /SCWM/OUT_PRD_DLV_FO of the ODFO item type. 

Can anyone let me know which item type need to be used for KIT to Order process without VAS in EWM which support Transport driven ASR process. 

Regards,

Milind

#S/4HANA2025, SAP Extended Warehouse Management SAP Transportation Management

entry.title: PI tolerance Warning Message During Count
entry.content:
Hello SAP Team, 

While performing physical Inventory count through RF, we are receiving tolerance group warning messages for every time the count is deviated from the actual book inventory.

While the Tolerance is within the limit, still the warning message appears every time and interrupting the counting process. Kindly suggest any solutions which can help us in preventing such warning messages during PI count. 

Error Message: Entered Value exceeds tolerance; check your count entry

Message Class: /SCWM/RF_DE066

entry.title: Error when using BSUoM on MM to do a outbound task
entry.content:
We work with materials whose basic unit of measure is piece, and we also use an alternative product unit of measure (BSUoM). The 2025 version allows only one batch characteristic.

We have a sales scenario where the sales unit is different from the basic unit of measure, using multiple alternative units. This scenario is possible in SAP 2020, where EWM works only with the basic unit of measure.

Has anyone encountered this issue in SAP 2023 or 2025? What was the solution?

An additional point is that we have the new "Batch Specification UoM" flag in EWM, which can be activated or deactivated via SPRO. However, when it is deactivated, it creates queues in the delivery integration with EWM. When it is activated, only deliveries using the basic unit of measure or a single alternative unit can be integrated.

Can anyone help?

entry.title: creating a follow-on HU Warehouse Task after Warehouse Task/Warehouse Order confirmation in EWM
entry.content:
Hi All,

We have a business requirement to create a follow-on HU Warehouse Task after Warehouse Task/Warehouse Order confirmation.

Business Requirement - Business requires a two-step put away process:

  1. Move the HU from the source bin to an Interim Bin
  2. Move the HU from the Interim Bin to the PSA

Instead of creating the Warehouse Task directly to the PSA, the stock should first be staged in the interim location. The destination bin is changed from the PSA to the Interim Bin using the CHBD exception code during Warehouse Task confirmation.

The requirement is to automatically create a second HU Warehouse Task that moves the HU from the Interim Bin to the PSA immediately after the first Warehouse Task is confirmed.

Approaches Evaluated :

  1. POSC / LOSC - We evaluated both POSC and LOSC. However, due to business and operational constraints, these approaches are not suitable for our process.
  1. Pick Point (Pick HU) Process - We configured the Interim Bin as a Pick Point. The system correctly creates two Warehouse Tasks (Product WT and HU WT). However, after business review, this solution was rejected because of several operational limitations and it is not considered practical for day-to-day warehouse execution.

Technical Options Explored :

Option A – BADI -  /SCWM/EX_CORE_CO_POST

This BADI is triggered during HU Warehouse Task confirmation.

The issue is that at this point, the Warehouse Task confirmation status has not yet been committed to the database. We considered calling the creation logic in an Update Task, but creating WH Task in an Update Task appears to be a heavy processing activity and is generally not recommended.

Option B – PPF

We looked for a PPF Action Profile but could not find any standard PPF action that executes after WT/WHO confirmation.

Option C – Asynchronous Background Process

Schedule a custom background job after confirmation that executes logic similar to the /SCWM/ADHU API.

Although technically feasible, the drawback is the delay between Warehouse Task confirmation and creation of the follow-on Warehouse Task. Ideally, the second HU Warehouse Task should be created immediately.

Has anyone implemented a similar requirement?

Is there any standard enhancement point, framework, or recommended approach to automatically create a follow-on HU Warehouse Task immediately after Warehouse Task/Warehouse Order confirmation, without relying on POSC/LOSC or asynchronous background processing?

Any suggestions or implementation experiences would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you in advance for your guidance.

SAP Extended Warehouse Management 

Regards,

Karthik

entry.title: SAP CPI OData V4 POST – Invalid CSRF Token Error for WarehouseTask Confirm API (EWM A2X)
entry.content:
Hi,

I am currently working with the standard SAP EWM API – Warehouse Order and Task (A2X), specifically using the WarehouseTask entity. This is an OData V4 API.

🔹 Scenario

I am trying to perform a POST operation to confirm a Warehouse Task using the below endpoint:

/sap/opu/odata4/sap/api_warehouse_order_task_2/srvd_a2x/sap/warehouseorder/0001/WarehouseTask/EU20/1234563436/0/SAP__self.ConfirmWarehouseTaskHndlgUnit

Where:

  • EU20 → EWM Warehouse
  • 1234563436 → Warehouse Task
  • 0 → Warehouse Task Item

🔹 Approach Followed

  1. First, I executed a GET call on the same API to:

    • Fetch the CSRF token
    • Retrieve the corresponding ETag for the specific combination (Warehouse, WarehouseTask, WarehouseTaskItem)in the body.

  2. For the POST request:

    • I used the same CSRF token in the header
    • Set the header:
      If-Match = <ETag (from GET response)>

🔹 Issue Faced

While triggering the POST request, I am receiving the following error:

Invalid CSRF token

🔹 Observed Behavior

  • In the CPI trace logs (receiver side), I can see that the CSRF token is being passed in the request header.
  • However, after the failure:
    • In the request headers attachment, the CSRF token appears as "fetch" instead of the actual token value.

This is creating a confusing situation, as the token seems to be passed but still results in an "Invalid CSRF token" error.

🔹 API Reference

For reference, I am using the API from SAP Business Accelerator Hub:
Warehouse Task Confirm API

🔹 Ask

Has anyone encountered a similar issue while working with this API or OData V4 CSRF handling in CPI?

Any guidance on:

  • Correct CSRF handling approach, or
  • Possible reasons why the token is getting replaced with "fetch"
    would be really helpful.

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: How to change the serial number user status RECG
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

We are currently facing an issue with a serial number status. In IQ02, one of the serial numbers is stuck with the user status RECG (“Product Recognition”), and we have been unable to remove or change this status despite trying multiple approaches.

Could you please advise where and how this status can be modified or removed?

Thanks,

Vinod

entry.title: Change an HU number range from internal to external?
entry.content:
What steps should be performed to change an HU number range from internal to external?

Between all the HU ranges configured in my warehouse, i selected an external one to be internal and test a process. This can't be undone, thus the message says that to delete it, the counter should be initial, therefore, that rage can no longer be used for external HU's. I seek to reset it as external Instead of re-configuring a new number range.

Does the counter resets by deleting HU's? I don't think so. 

Thank you all beforehand.

entry.title: Automatic block stock SAP EWM
entry.content:
Hello,

Is there a possibility to change / block stock of certain articles when they are internaly moved to a certain storage bin , or when a special process type is being used. The goal is to change stock type from unrestriced to blocked. 

Target system: SAP EWM decentral

entry.title: LOSC Putaway: Second WT Gets Cancelled After Confirming First WT
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I am testing the LOSC process for putaway in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud - EWM.

Configuration

  • GR Zone → Intermediate Storage Type → Putaway Storage Type

  • Created two storage types:

    • Intermediate Storage Type

    • Putaway Storage Type

  • Configured the required storage bins and bin determination.

  • Configured a simple LOSC process.

Test Scenario

After posting the Goods Receipt,  created the putaway Warehouse Task.

As expected, the system created two Warehouse Tasks:

  • First WT – Open

  • Second WT – Waiting

However, after confirming the first WT, the second WT is cancelled automatically, and the HU remains stuck in the intermediate storage bin 

Comparison with Outbound LOSC

For the outbound LOSC scenario, I used the same intermediate storage type with the following process:

Putaway Storage Type → Intermediate Storage Type → GI Zone

In this case, the system also created two Warehouse Tasks. After confirming both tasks, the HU moved to the GI Area successfully.

I also tested an outbound LOSC process with multiple intermediate storage types, and it worked as expected.

The issue occurs only during the putaway LOSC process.

Has anyone encountered this behavior before, or is there any additional configuration required for inbound LOSC in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud EWM?

Any guidance or suggestions would be greatly appreciated.
Thank you!

entry.title: Generate Warehouse Tasks
entry.content:
Hello,

Can you help us with the following theme, please?

We have a periodic job, program /SCWM/R_DLV_PDO_CREATE_WT (execution variant “FLUXO_CHAO_TDS”), the objective is to generate Warehouse Tasks for a specific delivery type(ZTRA). Our problem is with the sequence of selection of the deliveries(it doesn’t follows a number or date sequence of the deliveries), also it generates tasks in a non-arbitrary logic.

 

For example, he have 10 deliveries(from 1 to 10, all of them have the same dat), the program generates tasks for delivery 2, then goes to delivery 5, 8, then delivery , which means it doesn’t take a sequence. Also the tasks aren’t generated in the sequence of the delivery, it generates some tasks for delivery 2(doesn’t generates tasks for all the quantity), then goes for delivery 5(again generates tasks, but not for all the quantity), then goes to delivery 8(does the same thing),

For more details can you check the attached word document.

Thank you, best regards.

Diana Arana
